INTRODUCTION 
The System Validation Engine is designed using the concept of Turing Machines (TM) from 
Formal Languages and Automata Theory (FLAT). A Turing Machine is a powerful

Formal Languages and Automata Theory (FLAT). A Turing Machine is a powerful 
computational model capable of performing complex symbolic manipulations on an infinite 
tape. In this project, it is applied to solve a fundamental verification problem: checking whether 
two input strings separated by the symbol “#” are identical. 
String comparison is an important operation in many computational systems, especially in data 
validation, compiler design, and security applications. Unlike simple pattern matching 
techniques, a Turing Machine provides a step-by-step formal mechanism to process and verify 
strings systematically using state transitions and tape operations.

strings systematically using state transitions and tape operations. 
In this System Validation Engine, the Turing Machine reads input strings of the form w#w, 
where the left and right substrings are compared symbol by symbol. The machine marks 
matched symbols and continues the comparison until all characters are verified. If all 
corresponding symbols match, the machine accepts the input; otherwise, it rejects it. 
This project demonstrates the practical application of theoretical computation models in 
solving real-world validation problems and highlights the importance of automata theory in 
system design and verification processes. 
 
PROBLEM STATEMENT

system design and verification processes. 
 
PROBLEM STATEMENT 
The problem addressed in this System Validation Engine is the design of a Turing Machine 
that can determine whether two strings separated by the symbol “#” are identical. 
Given an input string of the form: 
                                                w # w 
the system must verify whether the substring before “#” is exactly the same as the substring 
after “#”. 
Traditional string comparison methods rely on high -level programming constructs, but this 
project aims to solve the same problem using a formal computational model. The challenge is 
to design a Turing Machine that can:

to design a Turing Machine that can: 
• Read symbols from both sides of the separator “#”  
• Compare them one by one  
• Mark matched symbols  
• Detect mismatches  
• Reach an accept or reject state accordingly 
 
 
OBJECTIVE 
The objectives of the proposed Automata-Based Multi-Layer Authentication System Using 
DFA and Turing Machine are as follows: 
• To design and implement a secure authentication system using concepts from Formal 
Languages and Automata Theory (FLAT). 
• To develop a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA)  for validating user login 
credentials through state transitions. 
• To design a Turing Machine (TM) for processing and verifying One-Time Passwords 
(OTP).

• To design a Turing Machine (TM) for processing and verifying One-Time Passwords 
(OTP). 
• To implement a Multi-Layer Authentication (MLA)  framework consisting of 
credential verification, OTP verification, and behavioral biometric verification. 
• To demonstrate the practical application of DFA and Turing Machine concepts in real-
world cybersecurity and authentication systems. 
 
THEORY / MODEL USED 
The System Validation Engine is developed using the concepts of Formal Languages and 
Automata Theory (FLAT) , specifically based on the computational model of a Turing 
Machine (TM) . The Turing Machine is a powerful theoretical model introduced by Alan

Machine (TM) . The Turing Machine is a powerful theoretical model introduced by Alan 
Turing, capable of simulating any algorithmic computation using an infinite tape, a tape head, 
and a set of states. 
In this project, the Turing Machine is used to solve the problem of string equality checking, 
where two strings are separated by the symbol “#”. The machine processes the input step by 
step, compares corresponding symbols from both sides, and determines whether they are 
identical. 
 
Turing Machine (TM) Model 
A Turing Machine is formally defined as: 
𝑀 = (𝑄, Σ, Γ, 𝛿, 𝑞0, 𝐵, 𝐹) 
 
Where: 
• Q → Finite set of states  
• Σ (Sigma) → Input alphabet

𝑀 = (𝑄, Σ, Γ, 𝛿, 𝑞0, 𝐵, 𝐹) 
 
Where: 
• Q → Finite set of states  
• Σ (Sigma) → Input alphabet  
• Γ (Gamma) → Tape alphabet (includes input symbols + special markers)  
• δ (Delta) → Transition function that defines state changes  
• q₀ → Initial state  
• B → Blank symbol  
• F → Set of final (accepting) states  
 
 
DESIGN / DIAGRAM 
The proposed System Validation Engine is designed using a Turing Machine (TM) model 
to perform string equality checking for two strings separated by the symbol “#” . The 
system follows a structured mark-and-compare approach, where each symbol from the left 
and right substrings is matched sequentially using tape operations and state transitions.

and right substrings is matched sequentially using tape operations and state transitions. 
The input string is processed on an infinite tape, and the Turing Machine uses a finite control 
unit to guide transitions between states. The system ensures that both strings are identical by 
marking matched symbols and rejecting any mismatch. The overall system architecture and 
working flow are illustrated below. 
 
Fig. 1. Overall System Architecture

Figure 1 represents the architecture of the System Validation Engine based on a Turing 
Machine model. The system consists of the following components: 
• Input String Tape containing data in the form w#w  
• Turing Machine Head for reading, writing, and moving across the tape  
• Finite Control Unit (State Transition System)  
• Tape Alphabet including input symbols and marking symbols (X, Y)  
• Decision Module (Accept / Reject State)  
The system operates by reading the input string, marking matched symbols, and comparing 
corresponding characters on both sides of the separator “#”. The process continues until all

corresponding characters on both sides of the separator “#”. The process continues until all 
symbols are verified. If all symbols match, the system enters the Accept State; otherwise, it 
transitions to the Reject State. 
 
 
2. System Components 
The architecture consists of the following components: 
• Input Tape: Contains input in the form w#w  
• Tape Head: Reads and writes symbols and moves left/right  
• Finite Control Unit: Controls state transitions  
• Tape Alphabet: {0, 1, #, X, Y, □}  
• Decision Module: Accept or Reject state 
 
 
Working Principle 
• The input string is first written on the tape in the form w#w.  
• The Turing Machine head scans the tape from left to right.

• The Turing Machine head scans the tape from left to right.  
• The finite control unit decides transitions based on the current symbol.  
• Matching symbols are marked to avoid reuse.  
• The machine continues until all symbols are processed.  
• If successful → system enters Accept State.  
• If mismatch occurs → system enters Reject State. 
 
 
4. Formal Definition of Turing Machine 
You should include this (VERY IMPORTANT for marks): 
• Q = {q0, q1, q2, q3, q_accept, q_reject}  
• Σ = {0,1,#}  
• Tape alphabet = {0,1,#,X,Y,□}  
• Start state = q0  
• Accept state = q_accept  
• Reject state = q_reject 
 
5. Transition Function  
  
 
 
6. Tape Transitions

7. Conclusion 
The System Validation Engine demonstrates a clear and practical application of Turing 
Machine theory for solving the string equality problem. By systematically marking and 
comparing corresponding symbols on both sides of the separator “#”, the machine ensures that 
every character is verified accurately. Any mismatch or imbalance in length immediately leads 
to rejection, while complete successful matching results in acceptance. 
This approach highlights the power of formal automata in performing structured computation 
and validation tasks. It not only reinforces theoretical concepts from automata theory but also

and validation tasks. It not only reinforces theoretical concepts from automata theory but also 
shows their relevance in real-world computing problems such as data verification, parsing, and 
protocol validation. The model effectively bridges theory and practice, illustrating how 
complex logical decisions can be reduced to simple, rule-based state transitions.

ABSTRACT 
The rapid growth of urban transportation systems has led to the widespread adoption of multiple

vii  
ABSTRACT 
The rapid growth of urban transportation systems has led to the widespread adoption of multiple 
ride-hailing services, including cars, bikes, and auto -rickshaws. Despite the availability of these 
services, users often face challenges in comparing fares across different platforms in real time, 
leading to confusion and inefficient decision -making. The Smart Fare Comparison App is 
developed to address this issue by providing a unified and user-friendly platform that enables users 
to compare estimated fares for various transportation modes instantly. 
 
The system integrates advanced mapping technologies such as the Google Maps API and the

The system integrates advanced mapping technologies such as the Google Maps API and the 
Distance Matrix API to accurately determine the shortest route, distance, and estimated travel time 
between a given source and destination. Based on these parameters, the application applies 
predefined pricing algorithms tailored to different ride categories, ensuring realistic and reliable 
fare estimation. 
 
The application is developed using the MERN stack, which includes MongoDB, Express.js, 
React.js, and Node.js, enabling a scalable, responsive, and efficient architecture. The frontend 
offers an intuitive interface where users can easily input their travel locations, while the backend

offers an intuitive interface where users can easily input their travel locations, while the backend 
handles API requests, distance calculations, and fare computation seamlessly. 
 
By consolidating fare information into a single platform, the system helps users make cost -
effective and informed travel decisions, reduces the time spent switching between multiple 
applications, and enhances overall convenience. Future enhancements of the system include 
integration with real -time ride-hailing service APIs, dynamic pricing mechanisms, and AI -based 
fare prediction models to further improve accuracy and user experience. 
1  
 
1. INTRODUCTION

fare prediction models to further improve accuracy and user experience. 
1  
 
1. INTRODUCTION 
 
In recent years, urban transportation has undergone a significant transformation due to the rapid 
growth of digital technologies and the widespread adoption of smartphones. With the increasing 
population in cities, the demand for efficient, reliable, and affordable transportation has become 
more critical than ever. People rely heavily on various modes of transport such as taxis, auto -
rickshaws, and bike taxis for their daily commute. However, one of the major challenges faced by 
commuters is the lack of a unified platform to compare fares across different transportation

commuters is the lack of a unified platform to compare fares across different transportation 
options.Most existing ride -hailing services provide fare estimates only within their own 
platforms. As a result, users often need to switch between multiple applications to compare 
prices, which is both time -consuming and inconvenient. Additionally, fare structures vary based 
on distance, time, traffic conditions, and demand, making it difficult for users to make informed 
decisions quickly. 
 
The Smart Fare Comparison App is designed to address these challenges by providing a centralized 
system where users can compare fares for multiple transportation options in real time. By

system where users can compare fares for multiple transportation options in real time. By 
integrating modern web technologies and mapping services, the application enables users to enter 
their source and destination locations and instantly receive estimated fares for different ride 
categories such as cars, bikes, and autos. 
The system utilizes advanced APIs such as Google Maps API and Distance Matrix API to calculate 
accurate distances and travel durations. These APIs play a crucial role in ensuring that the fare 
estimation is as close to real -world pricing as possible. Based on the retrieved distance and time

estimation is as close to real -world pricing as possible. Based on the retrieved distance and time 
data, the application applies predefined pricing models to compute estimated fares for various 
vehicle types. 
 
This project is developed using the MERN stack, which consists of MongoDB, Express.js, 
React.js, and Node.js. The frontend is built using  React.js, providing a responsive and interactive 
user interface. The backend, powered by Node.js and Express.js, handles API requests, processes 
data, and performs fare calculations. MongoDB is used for storing user data and ride -related 
information, ensuring scalability and flexibility.

1.1 Problem Definition

information, ensuring scalability and flexibility. 
 
2  
 
1.1 Problem Definition 
 
In today’s fast-paced world, transportation plays a vital role in daily life. Despite the availability 
of multiple ride -hailing platforms, users face several challenges when it comes to selecting the 
most economical and convenient option. One of the primary issues is the absence of a unified 
platform that allows users to compare fares across different transportation modes in real 
time.Currently, users must open multiple applications individually to check fare estimates, which 
leads to increased effort and time consumption. Moreover, the pricing mechanisms used by these

leads to increased effort and time consumption. Moreover, the pricing mechanisms used by these 
platforms are often dynamic and influenced by factors such as traffic conditions, demand, and time 
of day. This makes it difficult for users to accurately estimate travel costs and choose the best 
option. 
 
1.2 Existing Applications 
 
Several applications and systems currently exist in the domain of transportation and navigation. 
Popular ride-hailing services such as Uber and Ola provide users with fare estimates and booking 
options. However, these platforms are limited to their own services and do not offer comparisons

options. However, these platforms are limited to their own services and do not offer comparisons 
with other providers or transportation modes.Similarly,  mapping services like Google Maps 
provide route information, distance, and estimated travel time. While these features are highly 
useful for navigation, they do not include fare estimation or comparison functionalities.Some 
third-party applications attempt to provide fare  comparisons, but they often rely on limited data  
or outdated pricing models, resulting in inaccurate results. Moreover, many of these systems lack 
real-time integration with mapping APIs, which reduces their reliability. 
1.3 Proposed System

real-time integration with mapping APIs, which reduces their reliability. 
1.3 Proposed System 
The Smart Fare Comparison App is designed to overcome the limitations of existing systems by 
providing a unified platform for comparing transportation fares. The system allows users to input 
their source and destination locations and instantly receive fare estimates for different vehicle 
types.The application integrates Google Maps API and Distance Matrix API to fetch accurate 
distance and travel time data. Based on this information, the system applies fare calculation 
algorithms to estimate prices for various transportation modes such as cars, bikes, and autos.One

algorithms to estimate prices for various transportation modes such as cars, bikes, and autos.One 
of the key features of the proposed system is its real -time processing capability. The application 
quickly retrieves data from APIs and displays results in a user -friendly format, enabling users to 
make decisions instantly.

3. DESIGN AND METHODOLOGY 
 
The design and methodology of the Smart Fare Comparison App focus on building a

6  
3. DESIGN AND METHODOLOGY 
 
The design and methodology of the Smart Fare Comparison App focus on building a  
scalable, efficient, and user-friendly system that can provide real-time fare comparisons using 
modern web  technologies and external APIs. This chapter explains the architectural 
design, system components, data flow, and methodologies used to develop the application. 
 
3.1 System Architecture 
 
The Smart Fare Comparison App follows a client-server architecture , which separates the 
frontend (user interface) from the backend (data processing and API handling). This separation 
ensures better scalability, maintainability, and performance.

ensures better scalability, maintainability, and performance. 
 
Figure 3.1.1 System Architecture 
 
1. Presentation Layer (Frontend) 
This layer is responsible for user interaction. It is developed using React.js and provides 
an intuitive interface where users can: 
• Enter pickup and destination locations

7  
 
• View calculated distances and travel time 
• Compare fares across different vehicle types 
• Interact with the application in real time 
The frontend communicates with the backend through RESTful APIs. 
 
2. Application Layer (Backend) 
The backend is developed using Node.js and Key responsibilities include: 
• Receiving user input from the frontend 
• Calling Google APIs to fetch distance and time 
• Applying fare calculation algorithms 
• Sending processed data back to the frontend 
The backend ensures efficient handling of asynchronous API calls and manages the business 
logic of the application. 
Express.js. It acts as the core processing unit of the system.

logic of the application. 
Express.js. It acts as the core processing unit of the system. 
 
 
3. Data Layer (Database & External APIs) 
This layer includes:

Algorithms Used 
1. Distance Calculation Algorithm 
Uses Distance Matrix API to compute: 
• Distance between locations 
• Estimated travel time 
 
2. Fare Calculation Algorithm 
The fare is calculated using a simple formula: 
Fare = Base Fare + (Distance × Rate per km) 
Example: 
• Car: ₹50 + ₹12/km 
• Bike: ₹30 + ₹8/km 
• Auto: ₹40 + ₹10/km 
 
3. API Handling Algorithm 
• Send request to API 
• Receive response 
• Parse JSON data 
• Extract distance & time

4.1 Location Input & Processing Module (React + Google Places API)

4. IMPLEMENTATION 
4.1 Location Input & Processing Module (React + Google Places API)  
Figure 4.1.1 represents the location input module, which acts as the entry point of the Smart Fare 
Comparison App. This module allows users to enter their pickup and destination locations through 
an interactive user interface developed using React.js. The system integrates the Google Places 
Autocomplete API to provide real -time suggestions as users type, improving accuracy and user 
experience.Once the user selects valid locations, the input data is captured and stored in  the 
application state. The module ensures that the entered locations are properly formatted before

application state. The module ensures that the entered locations are properly formatted before 
being sent to the backend  server. This preprocessing step is essential to avoid errors during API 
calls and to ensure consistent results from the distance calculation service. 
 
      Figure 4.1.1 Location  Input & Processing Module (React + Google Places API)

17  
 
 
4.2 Distance Calculation Module(Google Distance Matrix API)  
In Figure 4.2.1 the Distance Calculation Module is shown as the core component responsible for 
computing the distance and travel duration between the  source and destination. This module  uses 
the Google Distance Matrix API, which provides accurate and real -time information based on 
current traffic conditions and routing algorithms. 
The backend sends a request to the API  with the selected origin and destination. The API  returns 
structured data containing distance (in meters) and duration (in seconds). This data is then 
processed and converted into user-friendly units such as kilometers and minutes.

processed and converted into user-friendly units such as kilometers and minutes. 
This module plays a crucial role in ensuring that the fare estimation is accurate and reliable, as all 
subsequent calculations depend on the retrieved distance. 
 
Figure 4.2.1 Distance Calculation Modul
18  
 
4.3 Fare Calculation Logic (Dynamic Pricing Algorithm)  
The fare calculation module, which computes the estimated travel cost for different vehicle types 
such as car, bike, and auto. The system uses a predefined pricing model where each vehicle 
category has a base fare and a rate per kilometer. 
The general formula used is: 
Fare = Base Fare + (Distance × Rate per km) 
For example:

The general formula used is: 
Fare = Base Fare + (Distance × Rate per km) 
For example: 
• Car: ₹50 + ₹12/km 
• Bike: ₹30 + ₹8/km 
• Auto: ₹40 + ₹10/km 
The module receives the calculated distance from the previous step and applies these formulas to 
generate fare estimates. The results are structured in a way that allows easy comparison between 
different transport options.This module ensures transparency in pricing and helps users choose the 
most cost-effective option. 
 
                                 Figure 4.3.1 Fare Calculation Module

19  
 
4.4 Backend API Implementation (Node.js + Express) 
Backend API Module 
The backend API module, which acts as the central controller of the system. Built using Node.js 
and Express.js, this module handles communication between the frontend and external APIs.The 
backend receives location data from the frontend and processes it by calling the Google Distance 
Matrix API. Once the distance data is retrieved, it applies the fare calculation logic and sends the 
results back to the frontend in JSON format. 
The backend follows an asynchronous processing model, ensuring that API calls do not block

The backend follows an asynchronous processing model, ensuring that API calls do not block 
execution. This improves system performance and allows multiple users to access the application 
simultaneously. 
 
 
 
4.5 Database Implementation (MongoDB)  
 
MongoDB is used as the database for storing user queries and fare comparison results. Being a 
NoSQL database, MongoDB provides flexibility in storing data in a JSON-like format. 
 
The system stores details such as: 
• Pickup location 
• Destination 
• Distance 
• Calculated fares 
• Timestamp 
 
Using Mongoose, a schema is defined to maintain structure and consistency in stored data. This

• Timestamp 
 
Using Mongoose, a schema is defined to maintain structure and consistency in stored data. This 
allows efficient querying and retrieval of past records for analysis or future reference. 
The database module enhances the system by enabling data persistence and scalability. 
20  
 
4.6 Results Display Module (React Dashboard UI)  
The results display module, where the calculated fares are presented to the user. The frontend 
dynamically renders the results using React components, displaying each vehicle type along with 
its estimated price. 
The UI is designed to be clean and user-friendly, allowing users to easily compare fares at a glance.

The UI is designed to be clean and user-friendly, allowing users to easily compare fares at a glance. 
Additional information such as distance and estimated travel time is also displayed for better 
decision-making. 
The module updates in real time as new data is received from the backend, ensuring a seamless 
user experience. 
 
 
System Workflow Integration 
The complete workflow of the Smart Fare Comparison App is as follows: 
1. User enters pickup and destination locations 
2. Frontend sends data to backend 
3. Backend calls Google Distance Matrix API 
4. API returns distance and time 
5. Backend calculates fares 
6. Results are sent to frontend 
7. Frontend displays fare comparison

5. Backend calculates fares 
6. Results are sent to frontend 
7. Frontend displays fare comparison 
This integrated workflow ensures smooth communication between all system components.

5. TESTING AND RESULTS 
 
5.1 Testing  
The Smart Fare Comparison App was tested using a combination of unit testing and integration 
testing to ensure the accuracy and reliability of each module. Unit testing was performed on 
individual components such as location input handling, API request processing, and fare 
calculation logic. Integration testing was conducted to verify the interaction between the frontend 
(React), backend (Node.js), and the external Google Distance Matrix API.

(React), backend (Node.js), and the external Google Distance Matrix API. 
The testing process focused on validating: 
• Correct input handling for pickup and destination locations 
• Accurate distance retrieval from the API 
• Proper fare calculation for different vehicle types 
• Seamless communication between frontend and backend 
• Error handling for invalid inputs or API failures

Performance Analysis 
The system was evaluated based on response time and accuracy. The average response time for 
fare calculation was observed to be between 1–3 seconds, depending on network conditions and

fare calculation was observed to be between 1–3 seconds, depending on network conditions and 
API response time. The use of asynchronous API calls in Node.js ensures that the syste m 
remains responsive even under multiple user requests.  The accuracy of fare estimation was 
directly dependent on the precision of the distance provided by the Google Distance Matrix API, 
hich is highly reliable and widely used in real-world applications.

6. CONCLUSION AND FUTURE SCOPE 
 
 
6.1 Conclusion 
The Smart Fare Comparison App provides an effective and user -friendly solution to the common 
problem of estimating travel costs across different transportation options. With the rapid growth 
of urban transportation services, users often face difficulty in selecting the most economical and 
efficient mode of travel. This project addresses that challenge by offering a centralized platform 
that calculates and compares fares in real time.  The system integrates modern web technologies 
such as React.js for the frontend and Node.js with Express.js for the backend, ensuring a responsive

such as React.js for the frontend and Node.js with Express.js for the backend, ensuring a responsive 
and scalable architecture. By utilizing the Google Distance Matrix API, the application is able to 
retrieve accurate distance and travel time data based on real-world conditions. This data is then 
used to compute fare estimates using predefined pricing models for different vehicle types such as 
cars, bikes, and auto-rickshaws. 
 
One of the key strengths of the system is its simplicity and efficiency. The application requires 
minimal user input and provides instant results, making it highly accessible for everyday use. The

minimal user input and provides instant results, making it highly accessible for everyday use. The 
modular design of the system ensures that each component, including location input, API 
communication, and fare calculation, operates independently while maintaining seamless 
integration. 
 
The testing results demonstrate that the system performs reliably under various conditions, 
providing accurate fare comparisons with minimal response time. The use of asynchronous 
processing in the backend enhances performance and ensures smooth handling of multiple 
requests. Overall, the Smart Fare Comparison App successfully achieves its objective of

requests. Overall, the Smart Fare Comparison App successfully achieves its objective of 
delivering a real-time fare estimation and comparison platform. It improves decision -making for 
users, reduces uncertainty in travel planning, and showcases the practical application of web 
development and API integration in solving real-world problems. 
27  
  
 
 
6.2 Future Scope 
 
Although the Smart Fare Comparison App provides a strong foundation, there are several 
opportunities for future enhancements that can significantly improve its functionality and real -
world applicability.  One major improvement is the integration of real-time pricing from

world applicability.  One major improvement is the integration of real-time pricing from 
ride-sharing platforms. Currently, the system uses predefined fare models, but by connecting with 
live APIs from services such as cab providers, the application can provide dynamic and more 
accurate pricing based on demand, traffic conditions, and surge pricing 
 
Another important enhancement is the implementation of Automatic Location Detection using 
GPS. This would allow the system to automatically detect the user’s current location, eliminating 
the need for manual input and improving convenience.  The application can also be extended by

the need for manual input and improving convenience.  The application can also be extended by 
adding route optimization features. By suggesting alternative routes with lower travel time or cost, 
the system can further assist users in making better travel decisions. Integration with map 
visualization tools would enhance user interaction and provide a more intuitive experience. 
 
In addition, the system can be developed into a mobile application using frameworks such as React 
Native. This would increase accessibility and allow users to access the service on the go, which is 
essential for real -time travel planning.  Another potential enhancement is the inclusion of user

essential for real -time travel planning.  Another potential enhancement is the inclusion of user 
accounts and history tracking. By storing previous searches and travel data, the system can 
provide personalized recommendations and analytics, helping users understand their travel 
patterns and expenses 
 
Cloud deployment and scalability improvements can also be considered to handle a larger number 
of users and ensure high availability. Security enhancements such as authentication and data 
encryption can further strengthen the system.  In the long term, the Smart Fare Comparison App 
has the potential to evolve into a comprehensive travel assistant platform that not only compares

has the potential to evolve into a comprehensive travel assistant platform that not only compares 
fares but also provides booking options, route recommendations, and real-time traffic insights.
